user_query:
I need help turning our recent local initiative results into outreach and follow-ups. Please use the provided files under input/ and create the specified outputs under output/.

Inputs:
- input/initiatives.csv — initiative performance data.
- input/meeting_transcript.txt — internal discussion with action items and collaboration ideas.
- input/rough_email_draft.txt — a rambling draft to the property manager that needs rewriting.

Deliverables and requirements:
1) Compute metrics and write structured results:
   - Read input/initiatives.csv and compute, for each initiative, two new fields:
     • foot_traffic_delta_pct = ((period_foot_traffic - baseline_foot_traffic) / baseline_foot_traffic) * 100
     • sales_delta_pct = ((period_sales - baseline_sales) / baseline_sales) * 100
     Round both to one decimal place.
   - Add a performance_flag per initiative with these rules:
     • "positive" if both deltas ≥ 0.0
     • "negative" if both deltas < 0.0
     • "mixed" otherwise
   - Save a summary CSV to output/metrics/initiative_summary.csv with columns in this order: initiative_id, name, foot_traffic_delta_pct, sales_delta_pct, performance_flag.
   - Also save output/metrics/key_findings.json containing:
     • top_two_by_sales_delta: array of the two initiative_ids with the highest sales_delta_pct (descending)
     • worst_one_by_foot_traffic_delta: the initiative_id with the lowest foot_traffic_delta_pct
     • counts: object with keys positive, mixed, negative and their counts
     • overall_notes: one sentence summarizing the distribution (e.g., how many positive/mixed/negative)

2) Draft two new emails using those computed results:
   - output/emails/chamber_of_commerce_email.txt: A professional email to the local Chamber of Commerce requesting the May–July initiatives calendar and proposing two low‑lift collaborations explicitly mentioned in input/meeting_transcript.txt. In the body, cite (by name and exact % values from output/metrics/initiative_summary.csv):
     • The top two initiatives by sales_delta_pct
     • The single worst initiative by foot_traffic_delta_pct
     Ensure the percentages quoted match the values in output/metrics/initiative_summary.csv. Keep tone: constructive, concise (200–300 words), and community‑minded. Include a closing line referencing the attached summary (initiative_summary.csv).
   - output/emails/franchise_rep_email.txt: An email to our franchise field rep summarizing all initiatives with a concise bullet list where each line includes initiative name plus foot_traffic_delta_pct, sales_delta_pct, and performance_flag taken from output/metrics/initiative_summary.csv. Ask for guidance on co‑op fund eligibility for participating in upcoming Chamber activities. Keep tone: professional and confident, ~250–350 words. Include a line noting that detailed metrics are in output/metrics/initiative_summary.csv and key points in output/metrics/key_findings.json.

3) Rewrite the rough property manager email:
   - Using input/rough_email_draft.txt, produce output/rewrites/property_manager_email.txt as a concise, calm, and professional email. Keep the appointment details (date, time, location) exactly as stated in the draft and retain the two specific requests (A‑frame placement near the east entrance on weekends; clarifying customer parking discount signage). Remove blamey language, tighten structure, and limit to 180–220 words.

4) Prepare meeting notes and action items:
   - From input/meeting_transcript.txt, create output/notes/meeting_notes.md with sections: Summary, Decisions, Action Items (each item must include owner and due date if mentioned, else mark TBD), Open Questions, and Data checks. In Data checks, list every initiative_id mentioned in the transcript and append its foot_traffic_delta_pct and sales_delta_pct pulled from output/metrics/initiative_summary.csv to show cross‑validation.

General constraints:
- All percentages quoted in emails and notes must match the values in output/metrics/initiative_summary.csv (rounded as specified). If an initiative_id from the transcript is not in the CSV, note it under Open Questions.
- Ensure all specified output files are created with the exact paths and filenames listed above.
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
